steps.title: Track the data relationship
steps.content: The assertion links warmer collection months to longer life span. Because wing centroid size is used as a proxy for body size and correlates with life span, the needed data must connect a warmer month with a larger wing measurement.

steps.title: Use the month comparison
steps.content: Choice A compares sexes, not month temperatures. Choice C points to a larger female wing size in September than July, which does not support the warmer-month pattern. Choice D gives temperature only, without wing size. The useful comparison shows male wing size larger in July than in October. The answer is B.
